summary: Gruber responds to Trump's tweet suggesting the 2020 election be delayed, calling it the most historically significant and potentially consequential statement of his presidency. He explains that Congress set Election Day in 1845 and the president has no authority to change it. Gruber notes that the system held up as no Republicans backed the idea, with McConnell and McCarthy flatly rejecting it. He frames this as Trump testing the waters for ending democracy, but finds hope in the bipartisan rejection. Gruber connects the timing to the historic GDP collapse announced the same day, arguing Trump floated the idea because he cannot win a fair election. He concludes with a bold prediction of 1-in-3 odds Trump drops out before the Republican convention.
key_points: Trump has no legal authority to delay the election — Congress set Election Day in 1845 and Inauguration Day is enshrined in the 20th Amendment
The bipartisan rejection, including from McConnell and McCarthy, represents the system holding up against an authoritarian probe
Democracy through open and fair elections is the foundational principle upon which all other political issues depend
The timing was no coincidence — Trump floated the idea moments before confirmation of the worst economic contraction on record
Merely proposing to postpone the election makes a mockery of the oath of office to uphold the Constitution
money_quotes: This tweet is the low point in this whole years-long nightmare of a presidency, and yet simultaneously the reaction to the tweet is the high point.
Democracy is the singular most important concept in our society, culture, and Constitution. Everything else, no matter how important, is secondary.
With this tweet Trump is doing no less than testing the water for ending democracy.
It is what until four years ago we would rightly have considered a logical contradiction — a profoundly consequential inanity.
A probing hammer tap testing for cracks on the keystone of democratic rule.
Donald Trump cannot win an honest fair election held 96 days hence, and by law an honest fair election must be held in 96 days.
Donald Trump is, thus, desperate and alone.
Merely proposing postponing the election makes a mockery of it.
key_insight: Trump's suggestion to delay the election was an authoritarian probe testing for cracks in democratic rule, but the immediate bipartisan rejection showed the system — at least on this singular question — held.
